item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article comes from here and is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really really love this one but I'd love it more if it had a slight but on how the effect was discovered just since it's so specific. Still got my upvote though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me at the image caption.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a horrible image in my head of a headless scientist scuffling towards me down a hallway with their hand raised in the air and a smiley face drawn on it. *shiver*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On the contrary, permanent marker can last for a week, and I have photos to prove it. I'm not sure what I've done with the photos, but still…

And it would probably only take one case of a survived self-decapitation for the Foundation to get interested, but admittedly that would likely be a rare event.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
'roughly a week'?
Ok, make that 3 days or so, and I believe this could happen… I don't think someone can have a marker-drawing on his palm for a -week- unless they're actively trying to keep it there… And for sure, not often enough to attract the Foundation to investigate…

Other than that, holy hell, loved the idea XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
UPDATE ON HAND:

I haven't had a chance to upload all the pictures yet, but the face I drew on my hand has finally disappeared, after six days. So this confirms that it may be quite possible for SCP-1151's effects to manifest in the times specified in the article. I'm surprised it lasted this long, but it may well have lasted even longer if I hadn't needed to use my hand for stuff so much.

Though this still leaves the question of how the effect was discovered in the first place…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
UPDATE ON HAND:

The experiment's still going, three days in. The face is still definitely visible, although it's getting very worn. I'm a bit surprised it's still this clear, judging by the rate it was disappearing for the first day or so.

Sandbox updated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
UPDATE ON HAND:

It's still there after two days, but it's wearing away quite quickly, especially nearer the thumb.

New picture uploaded to sandbox.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a great old style SCP. Simple with an interesting effect.

item.title: Re: YOU DID IT
item.content:encoded:
YOU MANIACS! YOU ACTUALLY DID IT! DAMN YOU! DAMN YOU ALL TO HELL!

So, yeah, it's ok, I guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is very odd, and I like it a lot.

I think my favorite part of it is the effect you get if you read in order: first the picture caption & the containment procedures, then the description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aw shoot, you broke the curse. And I was saving the perfect picture I got from Reddit for a chance to break it myself. Still, I definitely upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This experiment is just fantastic.
Makes me feel all warm and fuzzy that you're actually using graph paper. It has always bugged me that there are no measuring instruments visible in the pictures, as that's something I think a scientific organisation would surely use.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
UPDATE ON HAND:

One day has passed since I drew a face on my hand, and it's already wearing out quite quickly. At this rate I doubt it'll last for a week.

I've uploaded the latest photograph to my sandbox, if you're interested.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The most horrific thing about this is that the Foundation keeps the headless instances alive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love this. Just straight up love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All these puns are leaving a black mark on the Foundation!

Ha ha! Pun kinghood is mine!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
but I can't suspend disbelief enough to believe that anyone would ever have activated this SCP's effects in the first place, much less drawn enough attention that the Foundation would have found out about it.

Yeah well, that seems to be the way it goes. It could be that the entire pack of markers had this effect, hell an entire CASE of markers, hundreds of them, would make you think that a smiley-face drawn on your palm was your actual god damn face, and just had an unusually strong ability to stay on the skin.

Out of the (just for random number) 500 markers that had that ability, exactly one person actually had the idea to do it in a silly way.

And I fully believe this is why the Foundation goes to great lengths to investigate ANY sort of unusual thing; just think of how many things on this site are triggered only when certain conditions are met, and think of how many of them are an unlikely set of conditions. We have stuff that may have sat around long enough to decay into dust, except that someone just happened to be in the right place, doing the right thing, at the right time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A part of me wants an experiment log. What if the head was surgically amputated to minimize blood loss? What if a person who just drew on their palm shook hands with another person? Would the effect transfer, etc (is it the marker or the ink?).

Also, what if you used it to draw a penis on someone's face? Do they become a politician?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, I pretty much agree on all points.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And just like that, Forlorn was compromised

EDIT: Also, I've changed the time before weirdness happens to make more sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good idea. I can't figure out how to upload images here, so I've put it in my sandbox instead. Apologies for the poor picture quality; it's hard to recreate a lighting studio only using two torches, a desk lamp, a length of string and some blutac.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Puntime is always what tickles me, hands down!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't forget to take pictures - one of a hand with a week-old face on it would look more convincing, especially if you take it against a metal tabletop or blue sheets or something like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That picture is a great source of mood whiplash. At first I was like "awww", but then I read the caption and had to do a double take. Good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Experiment time! Expect a report back in one week.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was just coming back to do hand puns. Gotta hand it to you for beating me. Too bad I missed the epic face off. I would love to go head to head with you guys in an epic battle of puns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My issue with that solution is then it becomes a "thing that makes you wanna use it for god-knows-why".

item.title: Re: YOU DID IT
item.content:encoded:
I'm tempted to downvote it just to keep the curse alive.

…

Nah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No need to lose your head over all this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is getting really out of hand, we should stop before this gets silly to save face.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article is incredibly cute. Upboated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh come on, stop palming this off to other people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah. I have the same reservations. It seems incredibly unlikely that this thing would've ever been discovered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, this pun-off is a real nailbiter, it's hard to finger a winner here. But I'm going to knuckle down and point out that this entire index of manual puns is a complete wrist of time in a digital age.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotta hand it to you, you coulda lost a lot of face with that comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please. Your bag of jokes isn't so handy now, is it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know what I'm saying. I know this site like the back of my hand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, that's a backhanded compliment if I ever heard one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh please, give it up and face the music.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I refuse to believe that at least a few kids wouldn't draw smiley faces on their hands.

Though the permanence thing is an issue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Talk to the hand, bro

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn it, Corbette, stop pussyfooting around and face the issue!

Gotta hand it to you though, you have a good grasp of the situation

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Stuff," yes. But not smiley faces. And I'm pretty sure that it'd wear off within a week, even on a really dirty kid who never washed, unless it was some kind of special super-permanent marker. Palms grab stuff a lot. There's friction. And we're always shedding skin.

I'll change to a non-vote anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Little kids (and…older kids) draw stuff on their hands all the time. And this could happen if 1) It was a really dirty kid or 2) Since they thought it was their real head, they wouldn't dare wash it off.

Also, the marker could be that permanent

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That marker must come in handy

NOW THAT'S WHAT I CALL A FACE PALM

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How many still had smiley faces on their hands a week later, though?

I'll switch from downvote to no vote, if you promise me that this is a thing people really do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How many people would leave the face on their hands for a week without washing it off?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is good, but I'd suggest adding some effect to the pen itself that makes you want to draw the smiley face on your palm. As is, its effect would almost never happen and it kinda subtracts from the creepiness overall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just think of the number of classmates in elementary school who drew smiley faces on their hands. Then I freak out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm pretty sure magic marker would wear off of a hand before a week was up. Even if it didn't, how often do people draw smiley faces on their palms with any particular magic marker? I do sort of like the rest of the piece, particularly the "will begin to believe that their head is a dangerous growth" part, but I can't suspend disbelief enough to believe that anyone would ever have activated this SCP's effects in the first place, much less drawn enough attention that the Foundation would have found out about it.

My issues, obviously, since everyone else seems to like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish I could upvote this twice. It's both funny and oddly frightening while not being something world-destroying. Also, that picture? I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not sure why, but I like that my prediction of "it makes you think it's your head, until you did" was slightly wrong in that it behaves like your head after decapitation. It's strange, but cool.

I'd rather see a picture of the marker itself, and the smiley face lower in the article. Feels more official.

item.title: YOU DID IT
item.content:encoded:
YOU BROKE THE CURSE OF 1151

THANK YOU

+1
